definition: A five-signal early-warning scoring system for EM currency crises with 80% hit rate when 4-5 signals present, 45% at 3 signals, and 10% at 0-2 signals, validated across 15+ episodes since 1980. False positive rate ~25-30%, typically because preemptive policy adjustment occurred. Currently 8-12 economies exhibit 3+ signals. Stronger for detection than timing.
evidence: 80% crisis rate at 4-5 signals, 45% at 3, 10% at 0-2
False positive rate ~25-30% typically from preemptive policy adjustment
Managed-float/peg regimes produce 30-60% discrete devaluations vs continuous adjustment in credible floaters
Pair_0 debate confirmed framework as more actionable and better validated than alternatives
open_questions: What are the specific 5 signals and their individual predictive power
Can timing be narrowed beyond within 24 months
Whether the false positive rate from preemptive adjustment means the framework causes its own failure
